vitals.time_limit: No strict time limit; broom wagon support

course_description.overview: Elite gran fondo featuring 165 km through Sierra de Aitana climbs used by WorldTour pros, with 3,350 m elevation gain and 4 timed segments on iconic mountain passes.

key_segments.name: Puerto de Tudons
key_segments.description: Highest point at 1,024 m, 603 m gain, up to 11% gradients; Premio de montaña.

key_segments.name: Puerto de Confrides
key_segments.description: 257 m gain to 970 m, avg 5.8%, max 12%; Premio de montaña by Victory Endurance.

key_segments.name: Puerto de Sa Creueta
key_segments.description: Gran Fondo exclusive, 624 m gain to 780 m, timed section avg 2.6%, max 14%.

key_segments.name: Coll de Rates
key_segments.description: Mítico climb, 350 m gain, avg 6.7%, max 14.1%; final timed segment with special prizes.

key_segments.name: Alto de Finestrat
key_segments.description: Opening climb, 202 m gain to 360 m, avg 5.3%, max 10%.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Brutal mountainous challenge with sustained climbs, steep ramps up to 14%, and high altitude exposure; designed for experienced cyclists training like pros.

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Alicante Airport (ALC), then drive or transfer to Callosa d'en Sarrià.
logistics.accommodation: Local hotels and rentals in Callosa d'en Sarrià and Costa Blanca area; book early for May event.
logistics.registration_details: Online via aitana-tour.com; includes rider pack with jersey, backpack, products, and Algar waterfalls entry.
logistics.bike_logistics: Compact crankset (50/34) and wide cassette (11-32+) recommended; on-site mechanical assistance and post-race service.
logistics.local_considerations: Prepare for sudden mountain weather changes; road rules apply as non-competitive sportive; UCI-sanctioned.

final_verdict.one_liner: Elite road sportive conquering Alicante's mythic climbs with pro-level suffering and support.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong amateur climbers and gran fondo enthusiasts seeking WorldTour training camp vibes.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the stunning scenery, timed challenges, and important rider experience.
pro_tips: Use compact gearing and wide cassette for 14% ramps.
Fuel early and manage effort on early climbs.
Pack layers for mountain weather shifts.
Join a club for group prizes and camaraderie.
